description: Bidirectional key rotation regression vector. Two operator signing keys with non-overlapping active windows; lock-receipt schema v5 (frozen at vector creation) payloads each signed by its corresponding key. Verification cases assert: pre-rotation receipt verifies with its original key (historical receipts remain valid forever per spec §4.2.4); post-rotation receipt verifies with the new key; cross-key verifications fail; Ed25519 verify itself has no forward lower bound (pipeline-level temporal binding is a separate enforcement layer). Used by both wallop_core (Elixir) and wallop_verifier (Rust) to confirm cross-language Ed25519 + JCS parity holds across rotation.

key_id_derivation.rule: key_id is the first 4 bytes (8 lowercase hex chars) of SHA-256(public_key) per spec §4.2.4. This vector pins the rule across implementations: Elixir SHA-256 and Rust SHA-256 must produce byte-identical key_id values for the same public_key.

op_key_post_rotation.note: DETERMINISTIC TEST KEYPAIR — derived from the seed string "wallop-key-rotation-vector-v1-post-rotation-seed" via SHA-256. NOT a real operator signing key. Safe to publish; unsafe to trust for any production verification.

op_key_pre_rotation.note: DETERMINISTIC TEST KEYPAIR — derived from the seed string "wallop-key-rotation-vector-v1-pre-rotation-seed" via SHA-256. NOT a real operator signing key. Safe to publish; unsafe to trust for any production verification.

verifications.name: historical receipt continues to verify with original key
verifications.rationale: Spec §4.2.4 revocation is forward-only. The pre-rotation key remains in the keyring after rotation; receipts signed during its active window continue to verify forever.

verifications.name: wrong-key — pre-rotation receipt against post-rotation key
verifications.rationale: Receipt was signed by the pre-rotation private key; verifying against an unrelated public key MUST fail. Catches verifiers that drop the key_id check or mis-thread the public key.

verifications.name: post-rotation receipt verifies with new key
verifications.rationale: After rotation, new receipts are signed by the new active key; verifier resolves the new key_id and the signature checks out.

verifications.name: wrong-key — post-rotation receipt against pre-rotation key
verifications.rationale: Receipt was signed by the post-rotation private key; verifying against the pre-rotation public key MUST fail. Catches resolver bugs that map a receipt to the wrong keyring row.

verifications.name: Ed25519 verify itself has no forward lower bound
verifications.rationale: Receipt locked_at (2026-03-15) is BEFORE op_key_post_rotation.inserted_at (2026-04-01). This is a structurally impossible state in production (you cannot have signed with a key that does not yet exist) but at the cryptographic primitive level the signature is valid. Pinning expect=pass here forecloses an implementation that silently adds a forward lower bound to Ed25519 verify itself; the pipeline-level temporal-binding step (spec §4.2.4) is the separate, higher layer that rejects such receipts.
